In [9]:
tech_name_VPset = ['erhu_vibrato','violin_vibrato', 'erhu_portamento', 'violin_portamento', 'opera_portamento']
print('%s types of playing techniques displayed: ' % (len(tech_name_VPset)))
print(*tech_name_VPset, sep = ", ")

5 types of playing techniques displayed: 
erhu_vibrato, violin_vibrato, erhu_portamento, violin_portamento, opera_portamento
In [12]:
# find all .wav and .csv files in subdirectories
base_dir = './VPset_audio/'
target = "*.wav"
wav_files = []   # create list

for path, subdirs, files in os.walk(base_dir):
    for name in files:
        if fnmatch(name, target):
            wav_files.append(os.path.join(path, name))   # add an element to the list
            
for k in range(len(wav_files)):
    wav_files[k] = wav_files[k].replace(base_dir, '')
In [13]:
for jj in range(len(tech_name_VPset)):
    target_class = tech_name_VPset[jj]
    
    target_num = 0
    target_idx = []

    # if filename include target_class, count in
    for k in range(len(wav_files)):
        if target_class in wav_files[k]:
            target_num += 1
            target_idx.append(k)


    k = 0 # kth example

    targ_file = base_dir + wav_files[target_idx[k]]
    sr, x = scipy.io.wavfile.read(targ_file)
    x = x.astype(float)
    if len(x.shape)>1: x = np.mean(x,1)
    D = librosa.amplitude_to_db(np.abs(librosa.stft(x)), ref=np.max)
    
    plt.subplots(nrows=1, ncols=2, figsize=(16, 4))
    y, sr = librosa.load(targ_file, sr=44100)
    ipd.Audio(y, rate=sr)
    
    plt.subplot(121)
    plt.plot(x)
    plt.title(target_class)

    plt.subplot(122)
    librosa.display.specshow(D, sr=sr, x_axis='time',  y_axis='log')
    plt.title('Log-frequency power spectrogram')
    
    plt.show()
